chunk_summary: This section discusses case-based nutrition strategies for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants with comorbidities, emphasizing tailored interventions for conditions like CHD, NEC, cholestasis, and BPD to optimize growth and reduce complications.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of comorbidity-driven nutrition adjustments in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Identify specific interventions for conditions like CHD, NEC, cholestasis, and BPD.
Learn to monitor and address micronutrient deficiencies and electrolyte imbalances.
Recognize the importance of multidisciplinary coordination in managing complex cases.
key_facts: Infants with CHD may require shorter, paced feeds and nutrient-dense formulas to prevent energy loss and aspiration risk.
Post-NEC infants with stomas need output monitoring, electrolyte checks, and micronutrient evaluation rather than frequent formula changes.
Cholestasis increases the risk of fat-soluble vitamin deficiencies; water-miscible vitamins A/D/E/K and INR/PT monitoring are essential.
BPD infants on loop diuretics may develop hypochloremic alkalosis; correcting sodium and chloride deficits can improve growth and reduce nephrocalcinosis risk.
Prolonged feeds in CHD infants can waste energy and increase aspiration risk, highlighting the need for feed pacing.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Management of the infant with evolving BPD (Brigham and Women’s Hospital guideline) emphasizes addressing electrolyte imbalances and reassessing diuretic dosing to improve growth outcomes.
chunk_heading: PRETERM/LBW/ELBW POST-DISCHARGE NUTRITION;CASE-BASED COMORBIDITY- DRIVEN NUTRITION DECISIONS;
